text.title: pytest Fixtures
text.summary: Fixture scopes (function/class/module/session), yield cleanup, conftest layering, factory fixtures, and autouse.

section.title: Fixture Scopes
section.rule: Default to function scope — it isolates tests and prevents state leakage.
Use session scope only for expensive, read-only resources (e.g., DB connection pool, loaded ML model).
A fixture can only depend on fixtures of equal or wider scope — never narrow scope from wide.

example.description: Session fixture depending on function-scoped fixture is invalid

section.title: Yield Fixtures for Cleanup
section.rule: Use yield fixtures instead of finalizers — the cleanup code is co-located with setup.

example.code: @pytest.fixture def tmp_file(tmp_path): path = tmp_path / "data.csv" path.write_text("col1,col2\n1,2\n") yield path # cleanup — runs even if test fails path.unlink(missing_ok=True)

section.title: conftest.py Layering
section.rule: Place shared fixtures in the nearest conftest.py — root conftest for global, subdir conftest for local.
Do not import conftest.py files directly — pytest discovers them automatically.

example.code: tests/ ├── conftest.py # db_session, settings, api_client ├── unit/ │ └── conftest.py # lightweight mocks only └── integration/ └── conftest.py # real DB fixtures

section.title: Factory Fixtures
section.rule: Use factory fixtures (functions returning factory functions) instead of returning pre-built objects.
Factory fixtures allow tests to customize the object while sharing setup code.

section.title: Autouse Fixtures
section.rule: Use autouse=True sparingly — only for cross-cutting concerns like resetting global state or patching env vars.
Document autouse fixtures in conftest.py with a comment explaining why they are automatic.
